perturbation.original_text: Subscription fee in Territory is to be determined based on market research performed by Distributor with pricing to be agreed upon jointly and in writing Each quarter, Distributor will make royalty payments to IMNTV based on a Structure as follows: 55% of subscriber revenue for the subscription based services broadcasting IMNTV content only, net of telecom percentage provided in accordance with this Agreement. ("Programming Payments"), subject to the following: The parties acknowledge that some mobile carriers may bill end users for a full month in a single billing, and that others may bill for partial months or pro-rate the billing of Recurring Subscribers. For each full month of Fees received from a Recurring Subscriber, Distributor will pay IMNTV based on the Rate Structure (the "Full Month Payment"). To the extent that Distributor receives a partial month's Fee or pro-rated Fee from a Recurring Subscriber as a result of a carrier's billing practices, Distributor will pay IMNTV a proportionate or pro-rated portion of such Full Month Payment based on the amount received by Distributor.
perturbation.changed_text: Subscription fee in Territory will be determined by Distributor. Each quarter, Distributor will make royalty payments to IMNTV based on a Structure as follows: a percentage of subscriber revenue for the subscription based services broadcasting IMNTV content only, net of telecom percentage provided in accordance with this Agreement. ("Programming Payments"), subject to the following: The parties acknowledge that some mobile carriers may bill end users for a full month in a single billing, and that others may bill for partial months or pro-rate the billing of Recurring Subscribers. For each full month of Fees received from a Recurring Subscriber, Distributor will pay IMNTV based on the Rate Structure (the "Full Month Payment"). To the extent that Distributor receives a partial month's Fee or pro-rated Fee from a Recurring Subscriber as a result of a carrier's billing practices, Distributor will pay IMNTV a proportionate or pro-rated portion of such Full Month Payment based on the amount received by Distributor.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of "to be agreed upon jointly and in writing" gives Distributor unilateral control over setting the subscription fee, contradicting the intended mutual agreement as indicated in the original text. Additionally, the omission of the specific percentage "55%" makes the royalty payment structure uncertain, creating an internal contradiction regarding how "a percentage of subscriber revenue" is to be determined. This impacts the later reference to the 'Full Month Payment' which no longer has a clear basis for calculation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Mobile Product Payments. Subscription fee in Territory will be determined by Distributor. Each quarter, Distributor will make royalty payments to IMNTV based on a Structure as follows: a percentage of subscriber revenue for the subscription based services broadcasting IMNTV content only, net of telecom percentage provided in accordance with this Agreement. ("Programming Payments"), subject to the following: The parties acknowledge that some mobile carriers may bill end users for a full month in a single billing, and that others may bill for partial months or pro-rate the billing of Recurring Subscribers. For each full month of Fees received from a Recurring Subscriber, Distributor will pay IMNTV based on the Rate Structure (the "Full Month Payment"). To the extent that Distributor receives a partial month's Fee or pro-rated Fee from a Recurring Subscriber as a result of a carrier's billing practices, Distributor will pay IMNTV a proportionate or pro-rated portion of such Full Month Payment based on the amount received by Distributor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for joint agreement on subscription fees, granting unilateral control to the Distributor. This directly contradicts the original intent of mutual agreement and creates uncertainty in the 'Full Month Payment' calculation. The omission of '55%' creates further contradiction as the payment structure is now undefined, impacting later references to this structure. The changed text now contradicts the notion of a pre-agreed upon structure elsewhere in the document.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor will provide IMNTV with a quarterly report or monthly report if available, summarizing the data relevant to the payment obligations herein within five (5) business days from the end of the calendar quarter. The reports will contain sufficient information to permit Provider to verify payments hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor will provide IMNTV with a quarterly report summarizing the data relevant to the payment obligations herein within a reasonable time from the end of the calendar quarter. The reports will contain information deemed appropriate by Distributor.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "monthly report if available" and changing "five (5) business days" to "a reasonable time", and "sufficient information to permit Provider to verify payments hereunder." to "information deemed appropriate by Distributor", the amendment introduces ambiguity regarding the frequency and content of the reports. This contrasts with the detailed payment terms outlined in Section 6.1. The changed text conflicts with the necessity for transparency and verifiability of payments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Mobile Product Payments. Subscription fee in Territory will be determined by Distributor. Each quarter, Distributor will make royalty payments to IMNTV based on a Structure as follows: a percentage of subscriber revenue for the subscription based services broadcasting IMNTV content only, net of telecom percentage provided in accordance with this Agreement. ("Programming Payments"), subject to the following: The parties acknowledge that some mobile carriers may bill end users for a full month in a single billing, and that others may bill for partial months or pro-rate the billing of Recurring Subscribers. For each full month of Fees received from a Recurring Subscriber, Distributor will pay IMNTV based on the Rate Structure (the "Full Month Payment"). To the extent that Distributor receives a partial month's Fee or pro-rated Fee from a Recurring Subscriber as a result of a carrier's billing practices, Distributor will pay IMNTV a proportionate or pro-rated portion of such Full Month Payment based on the amount received by Distributor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the reporting requirements, moving from specific deadlines ('five (5) business days') and content ('sufficient information to permit Provider to verify payments') to vague terms ('a reasonable time', 'information deemed appropriate by Distributor'). This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines a specific royalty payment structure ('a percentage of subscriber revenue') and expects accurate reporting to calculate those payments. The changes introduce ambiguity and give the distributor more control over payment information, which is incompatible with the contradicted section's expectation of verifiable payments.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate the Agreement at any time in the event of a material breach by the other party that remains uncured after thirty (30) days written notice of the breach.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate the Agreement at any time in the event of any breach by the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause "that remains uncured after thirty (30) days written notice of the breach", this makes any breach, even a minor one, grounds for immediate termination, contradicting the original intent of allowing a cure period for material breaches. This omission contradicts the overall spirit of a business agreement that aims for resolution and continuation of the partnership, creating uncertainty about what constitutes an acceptable minor or major breach that could lead to termination. This absence directly impacts Section 7.2, where specific actions and cure periods are defined only for Programming Deficiencies, not for all breaches.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination for Programming Deficiency. Distributor reserves the right to issue a warning immediately to Subscribers, to temporarily or indefinitely suspend the availability of the Programming, or to terminate this Agreement (the "Remedies") if IMNTV breaches this Agreement. In the event that Distributor exercises any of its rights set forth in this Section 7.2, Distributor will notify IMNTV of the Programming Deficiency, Distributor's elected Remedy, and the available cure options, and will provide IMNTV with a reasonable period of time in which IMNTV may cure the Programming Deficiency (the "Cure Period"). During the Cure Period, if commercially reasonable, the Distributor may help IMNTV cure the Programming Deficiency, but is under no affirmative obligation to do so. If IMNTV fails to cure the Programming Deficiency within the Cure Period, Distributor may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice. In the event of a suspension or termination under this Section 7.2, Distributor will notify Subscribers that the Programming is no longer available. Distributor will be solely liable for any refunds pertaining to the Programming that are issued to Subscribers as a result of termination under this Section 7.2.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the cure period for breaches, allowing termination for any breach, while the contradicted text, Section 7.2, specifically outlines a cure period for "Programming Deficiencies." The changed text directly contradicts Section 7.2 by eliminating the opportunity to cure breaches, rendering the cure period outlined in 7.2 meaningless and creating an in-text contradiction according to the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition, as the essential language regarding a cure period has been removed, creating conflict.
